channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Ecolab to buy KKR-backed CoolIT for $4.75 billion amid AI data center boom
item.description: March 20 : Ecolab said on Friday it would acquire KKR-backed CoolIT Systems for about $4.75 billion in cash, as it seeks to capitalize on surging demand for liquid cooling in artificial intelligence-driven data centers.Shares of the water management company w… [Intel]

item.title: Comparing AI chips: GPU, ASIC, and NPU
item.description: This article breaks down the three main categories of AI chips—GPUs, ASICs, and NPUs—and compares the leading hardware defining the future of artificial intelligence. You might think of NVIDIA as the undisputed king of artificial intelligence hardware, but th… [Intel, Nvidia]

item.title: AI startup Yotta seeks $4 billion valuation ahead of planned IPO
item.description: The Mumbai-based company is aiming to secure roughly $500 million to $600 million in capital and then file its formal listing documents within weeks, according to people familiar with the matter. It plans to raise a similar amount in the public offering.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang bids to own the entire AI factory stack
item.description: Nvidia dominated tech news this week, as its hold on the artificial intelligence factory boom only tightened at its annual GTC conference in San Jose. It introduced a raft of updated chips and software as well as partnerships with just about everyone in sight… [Intel, Nvidia]

item.title: Food Flavors Market to Register 4.9% CAGR Through 2031 Fueled by Natural Ingredient Trends and Rising Processed Food Demand, Reports Mordor Intelligence
item.description: The global food flavors market is projected to reach USD 27.12 billion by 2031 fueled by rising demand for natural ingredients, increasing processed food consumption, and continuous advancements in flavor technologies, with Asia-Pacific emerging as a key grow… [Intel]

item.title: $80 Billion Gone: What Was Meta’s Metaverse Project That Is Now Shut Down?
item.description: Tech giant Meta has reportedly announced that it is shutting down its metaverse project, which was a dream project of its founder Mark Zuckerberg, who had called it “the next frontier" upon rebranding the social media company from Facebook to Meta in 2021. Fo… [Meta, ServiceNow]

item.title: Bank Trojan Hits Android Users, Targets Payment System Used by 150,000,000 People
item.description: A new Android banking trojan that uses a mix of human and artificial intelligence agents to steal from victims is now targeting a payments system relied on by tens of millions of people. According to cybersecurity firm Zimperium, the PixRevolution banking mal… [Intel, ServiceNow]

item.title: Data Centers Are Military Targets Now
item.description: With militaries increasingly relying on artificial intelligence, data centers have emerged as new targets for strikes. The post Data Centers Are Military Targets Now appeared first on The Intercept. [Intel, ServiceNow]

item.title: HBO’s ‘The Pitt’ nails how hospital cyberattacks create chaos, endanger patients and disrupt critical care
item.description: Policy and legal efforts aim to stop these malicious security breaches, but they are growing more common as hospitals adopt remote health care delivery and hackers adopt artificial intelligence. [Intel]

item.title: SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic could be 3 of the biggest venture-backed IPOs of all time
item.description: If all three went public it could “conceivably create more value than all VC-backed IPOs since 2000 have collectively.” [OpenAI, Anthropic]
